title: Preparing to Install and Run Uniface on Unix and Linux
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Unix and Linux Platforms
Preparing to Install and Run Uniface on Unix and Linux
text: Preparing to Install and Run Uniface on Unix and Linux When installing Uniface on Unix platforms, you need to create an environment for Uniface. If the Uniface Router and the Uniface Server are to run under different accounts, the Uniface Router needs to run under the root account. Create a user ID for Uniface, for example, user uniface , or use an existing account. Create a home directory for this user. The location suggested by the installer is in the home directory of the user running the installer. You can keep this suggestion or change it to another location to which this user has write access. Set up the user environment for running Uniface after installation: Make sure that any setup scripts for DBMS, network, or GUI products are executed. Depending on your system, setup scripts might already exist in the global profile for all users. If not, you can put them in the .profile file for the Uniface user account. Consult your DBMS documentation for information on any quotas that need to be enabled for the Uniface account. Install and configure the Sentinel RMS License Manager, if you are using Network licensing. For more information, see Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux . Note: This is not required if you are using Sentinel cloud-leased licenses on Linux. In this case, the license environment is automatically configured as part of the installation process. Related Links Installing Uniface on Unix and Linux The Uniface installation program and related files are provided in a .tar file. After extracting the file you can run the Uniface installer.
